message:
perf(vdbe): promote Rowid into try_execute_hot_opcode hot-path

Rowid extracts the rowid of the currently-positioned row on cursor p1
into register p2. Emitted at ~51 codegen sites across fsqlite-vdbe/
-planner/-core, driving every JOIN match-probe rowid lookup, every
UPDATE/DELETE row-key read, every INSERT-returning-rowid path, every
rowid-as-column projection, and the by-rowid aggregate fast paths.

Body mirrors the existing main-match arm verbatim: single
`cursor_rowid` call (one HashMap probe in the common storage-cursor
path, one cursor.rowid call) plus a register write. Same family as
the already-promoted IsNull/IfPos/IfNot/SCopy: small body, high call
frequency, dispatch routing cost dominates work cost.

Bench (vdbe_pipeline_execute_rowid, criterion p<0.05):

| variant | before  | after   | Δ thrpt |
|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| /64    | 3.27 µs | 3.20 µs | +2.4%  |
| /256   | 9.29 µs | 8.88 µs | +4.6%  |
| /1024  | 33.34 µs| 31.53 µs| +5.4%  |

Cross-check sanity-rerun of the restored after-state against the
original after baseline showed an additional -4.0% to -4.5% drift,
which would put the conservative win at ~9-10% in the larger sizes
— but the directional signal is already clear from the primary A/B.
Smaller delta than IsNull/IfPos because Rowid's body involves a
HashMap probe + cursor.rowid() call, so dispatch is a smaller
fraction of total per-op cost.

Test (test_rowid_via_hot_path): pins the hot-path arm's behaviour
to the main-match arm across the two Rowid input classes that share
a single `cursor_rowid` call: storage cursor positioned on a row
emits Integer(rowid); never-opened cursor id emits Null.

Bench (vdbe_pipeline_execute_rowid): same shape pattern as the
IsNull/IfPos/IfNot benches — single positioned cursor, op_repeats
back-to-back Rowid ops in the inner loop, isolating the hot-path
pre-filter vs main-match routing cost from cursor motion.

Layout note for both test and bench: the bytecode verifier requires
Rewind p2 to be strictly `< op_count`, so the Rewind EOF target
points at a real instruction (the program-end Halt) via a label
resolved *at* that Halt. Init is omitted: the engine starts at
pc=0 unconditionally and no verifier path requires Init's presence.

Test suite: 735→736 (+1 new, 0 regressions); 45 pre-existing
failures unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
